title: practical english usage - Part 166
content:
leave from somewhere (talking about the place) We'll laugh about this one day.

I hate being laughed at.

Your mother lacks tact.

[to] lack (no preposition) She is lacking in tact.

Lack of time prevented me from writing.

People have always been very kind to me.

interested to do /in doing something: ►     105.16 Not many people are interested in grammar.

When did your interest in social work begin?

interest/interested in (     far) George’s father insisted on paying.

When did India get its independence from Britain?

She got a job so that she could be independent of her parents.

independent, independence of or from I’d like to see a big increase in productivity.

increase in activity, output, etc (     ef) I’m  very impressed with/by your work.

The boss has been ill with bronchitis this week.

I don’t like the idea of getting married yet.

[the] idea of.. .ing (     the idea to-...) Are you any good at tennis?

We’ll be getting off the train in ten minutes.

get on(to) and off a train, plane, bus, ship, (motor)bike or horse VVTzen / got into my car, I found the radio had been stolen.

get in(to) and out of a car, taxi or small boat frightened of or by: ►    96.9.

As Schiller said, against stupidity even the gods fight in vain.

fight, struggle, etc against something Section Prepositions               18 grammar •  213   prepositions after particular words and expressions Emily shouted to us to come in and swim.

shout to (= call to) If you don't stop shouting at me, I'll come and hit you.

shout at (aggressive) I was terribly shocked at/by the news of Daniel's accident.

They searched the man in front of me from head to foot.

They searched everybody's luggage.

search (without preposition) (= look through; look everywhere in/on) The customs were searching for drugs at the airport.

search for (= look for) I ran into Philip at Victoria Station this morning.

Peggy  was pretty rude to my family last weekend.

Who's responsible for the shopping this week?

responsible/responsibility for (     of) She reminds me of a girl I was at school with.

remind of (and ►    568) Nobody knows the reason for the accident.

reason for (     of) I want proof of your love. Lend me some money.

The noise from downstairs prevented me from sleeping.

prevent... from .. .ing (     to) Try to be polite to Uncle Richard for once.

polite to (     with) I wasn't very pleased with/about/at my exam results.

pleased with/about/at something The boss is very pleased with you.

pleased with somebody Excuse me, sir. You haven't paid for your drink.

pay for something that is bought (     pay something) They operated on her yesterday evening.

operate on a patient You weren't very nice to me last night.

near (to): ►    531.

She married her childhood sweetheart.

marry somebody (no preposition) How long have you been married to Hannah?

Her marriage to Philip didn't last very long.

marriage to; get/be married to (     with) make, made of/from: ►    522.

Can you help me look for my keys?

look for (= try to find) grammar *214  expressions without prepositions the need...) not The Minister emphasised the need for secrecy, (     .. .-emphasised-on not We must discuss your plans, (     .. . discuss about your plans?) These verbs are normally followed by direct objects without prepositions.

1  discuss,        emphasise,           enter,      marry,       lack,     resemble          and   approach or can leave them out.

This is a list of some common expressions in which  we  do not use prepositions, 214 expressions         without      prepositions        _________ For of after determiners like some, most, ►    Section 13.

What's wrong with Rachel today?

write: ►    8.

The wine's typical of the region.

typical of (     for) He tripped over the cat and fell downstairs.

Could you translate this into Greek for me?

translate into (     hi) If you get the ball, throw it to me.

throw... to (in a game, etc) Stop throwing stones at the cars.

throw... at (aggressive) I hate the thought of going back to work.

the thought of (     theAheughHo) I've also thought about studying dentistry.
